summary: Comprehensive end-to-end testing of Users entity (backend + frontend): admin/users.py, permissions.py, router conflict analysis, permission matrix, tenant isolation, security hardening. 45 backend tests passed, 16 skipped (idempotent). Frontend UI fully functional with 2 UI bugs found.

critical.issue: require_super_admin security bypass: checks 'role not in super_admin_roles AND NOT is_admin'. The OR condition means ANY is_admin=True user (partner_admin, partner_staff) bypasses super-admin check. CONFIRMED: partner_admin and partner_staff can deactivate users and read audit logs.
critical.fix: In security.py require_super_admin (line ~116): Remove the 'or not user.get("is_admin")' OR condition. Check role explicitly: 'if user.get("role") not in super_admin_roles: raise 403'

critical.issue: platform_admin NOT in has_permission() super_admin check. permissions.py has_permission() checks 'platform_super_admin' (not 'platform_admin'). CONFIRMED: platform admin receives 403 when calling DELETE /api/admin/users/{id}.
critical.fix: In permissions.py has_permission() (line ~323): Add 'platform_admin' to the super_admin_roles set alongside 'platform_super_admin'

critical.issue: No password complexity validation for admin user creation. CONFIRMED: weak password 'weak' is accepted (201). The _validate_password_complexity() function exists in auth.py but is NOT called in admin_create_admin_user().
critical.fix: In admin/users.py admin_create_admin_user(): Add _validate_password_complexity(payload.password) call before creating user. Import the function from auth module or move it to a shared util.

minor.issue: token_version is never incremented. The token_version check EXISTS in get_current_user() (security.py line ~100-103) but NO endpoint increments it on password change/reset. Old tokens remain valid after password reset.
minor.fix: In the password reset endpoint: After hashing new password, increment token_version: $inc: {token_version: 1}

minor.issue: No orphan protection: Deactivating the ONLY active partner_super_admin in a tenant is NOT blocked. A partner_admin (via is_admin bypass) can deactivate the super_admin, leaving the tenant unmanageable.
minor.fix: Before deactivation: check if user has role=partner_super_admin AND count active partner_super_admins in tenant. If last one, return 400 'Cannot deactivate the only super admin'

minor.issue: users.py PUT route uses get_tenant_admin (any admin passes) without checking has_permission('users', 'edit'). Any partner_staff can edit any admin user without 'users' module permission. CONFIRMED: partner_staff can call PUT successfully.
minor.fix: Add has_permission check in admin_update_user() in users.py: check 'users' module permission before allowing edit

minor.issue: ARCHITECTURAL: Both admin/users.py and permissions.py register GET+POST /api/admin/users. admin_users_router registered first (server.py line 132) wins. permissions.py list_admin_users and create_admin_user are DEAD CODE (never reached). The two endpoints have different behavior (users.py: role-based with pagination; permissions.py: module-based without pagination).
minor.fix: Consolidate: Either (1) Remove duplicate routes from permissions.py and redirect to users.py, or (2) Add unique path prefix like /api/admin/users/permissions-based. Currently permissions.py POST create_admin_user is completely unreachable.

ui_bugs.issue: Super admin users (partner_super_admin role) show '● Read' access badge instead of '● Full'. This is because they don't have access_level='full_access' field set (created via tenant endpoint). Display code only checks access_level field, not role.
ui_bugs.fix: In UsersTab.tsx line ~227-228: Change condition to: {(u.role === 'partner_super_admin' || u.role === 'super_admin') ? 'Full' : (u.access_level === 'full_access' ? 'Full' : 'Read')}

ui_bugs.issue: Logs dialog does not have data-testid='admin-user-logs-dialog' attribute. Testability gap.
ui_bugs.fix: In UsersTab.tsx logs dialog: Add data-testid='admin-user-logs-dialog' to the Dialog/DialogContent component

integration_issues.issue: The Create User dialog does NOT have a 'Role' dropdown for partner_super_admin/partner_admin/partner_staff roles. UI only supports preset_role templates and module-based creation. Users created via UI always get role='admin' (users.py default). The 'role-based' creation (partner_super_admin/admin/super_admin) is only accessible via direct API calls.
integration_issues.note: This may be intentional UX decision but limits the ability to set specific roles via UI

issues: Super admin rows show 'Read' access badge (amber/eye icon) which looks like they have restricted access - misleading
The 'Only super admins can manage admin users' subtitle is accurate but may confuse users who see Edit buttons for non-super-admins

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix require_super_admin in security.py - remove is_admin=True bypass (ANY is_admin user can call super-admin endpoints)
HIGH: Fix has_permission() in permissions.py - add 'platform_admin' to super_admin_roles set
HIGH: Add password complexity validation in admin/users.py admin_create_admin_user()
MEDIUM: Increment token_version in password reset/change endpoint
MEDIUM: Add orphan protection - block deactivating last partner_super_admin in tenant
MEDIUM: Add has_permission('users','edit') check in users.py PUT /admin/users/{id}
MEDIUM: Resolve router conflict - consolidate GET+POST /api/admin/users routes (both users.py and permissions.py register same paths)
UI: Fix super admin Access badge display (shows 'Read' instead of 'Full' for partner_super_admin users without access_level field set)
UI: Add data-testid to logs dialog for testability
critical_code_review_comments: security.py require_super_admin (line ~116): 'not user.get("is_admin")' is a logic bug - should be 'and not user.get("is_admin")' evaluated correctly means ANY is_admin=True user bypasses role check. CONFIRMED working bypass.

permissions.py has_permission() (line ~323): super_admin_roles set contains 'platform_super_admin' not 'platform_admin'. These are two different roles. platform_admin is the actual role for platform admins (defined in constants.py PLATFORM_ROLE='platform_admin'). This mismatch means platform admins fail permission checks.
admin/users.py admin_create_admin_user(): Password validation missing. auth.py has _validate_password_complexity() but it's only called in customer/partner signup flows, not admin user creation.

server.py line 132 vs 153: admin_users_router included before permissions_admin_router. Both have GET /api/admin/users and POST /api/admin/users. FastAPI first-match wins. permissions.py create_admin_user() is unreachable dead code.
token_version in security.py: The check exists but no endpoint calls '$inc: {token_version: 1}' after password change. The feature is half-implemented.
seed_data_creation: Created test tenants: test-iter109-a, test-iter109-b. Created test users: TEST.super.a@iter109.test, TEST.super.b@iter109.test, TEST.admin.a@iter109.test, TEST.staff.a@iter109.test, multiple test admin users in both tenants. Tenants deactivated in cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test tenants test-iter109-a and test-iter109-b may still exist (deactivated). Test users with email pattern *@iter109.test exist in these tenants. Main security issues: (1) require_super_admin is_admin bypass CONFIRMED - partner_admin/staff can deactivate users; (2) platform_admin DELETE 403 CONFIRMED; (3) weak passwords accepted for admin creation CONFIRMED. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter109_users_permissions.py. Run with: pytest backend/tests/test_iter109_users_permissions.py -v. Idempotent - handles existing test data gracefully.

require_super_admin_bypass.root_cause: security.py require_super_admin condition: 'if user.get("role") not in super_admin_roles and not user.get("is_admin"): raise 403'. This means: PASS if role IS in super_admin_roles OR is_admin=True. Since all partner_admin and partner_staff users have is_admin=True in DB, they all bypass this check.
require_super_admin_bypass.fix: Change condition to: 'if user.get("role") not in super_admin_roles: raise 403' (remove is_admin bypass)

platform_admin_has_permission.root_cause: permissions.py has_permission() defines super_admin_roles as {'platform_super_admin', 'partner_super_admin', 'super_admin'}. The actual platform admin role constant (constants.py PLATFORM_ROLE) is 'platform_admin' (not 'platform_super_admin'). This name mismatch means platform admins fail all has_permission() checks.
platform_admin_has_permission.fix: Add 'platform_admin' to super_admin_roles set in has_permission(), or use PLATFORM_ROLE constant
